topic: GitHub Copilot Semantic Code Search: Find Code by Concept, Not Keyword

competitors.title: Copilot coding agent works faster with semantic code search - GitHub Changelog
key_points: Semantic code search tool allows Copilot to find relevant code based on meaning rather than exact text matches
Replaces reliance on tools like grep with vector similarity search
Completes tasks in 2% less time without quality degradation
No configuration required - agent automatically uses semantic search when appropriate
Available in GitHub Copilot v1.200+ VS Code extension

competitors.title: Semantic Search Architecture with GitHub Copilot — Production Best Practices [2026]
key_points: Production-grade semantic search architecture using vector databases (Qdrant v1.12 with HNSW index)
Embedding pipeline with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536 dimensions) for code snippets
40% better relevant context picks compared to BM25 keyword search
Sub-50ms query latency requirements for production deployment
Two-tier caching: local LRU (1GB per node) + Redis remote cache keyed by file hash
Embedding API latency dominates at 300ms p50, making caching highest ROI optimization

competitors.title: Continue.dev AI Assistant Market Analysis
key_points: $5.5B market in 2024 growing to $47.3B by 2034 (24% CAGR)
84% of developers use or plan to use AI coding tools
20M GitHub Copilot users, 4.7M paid subscribers (75% YoY growth)
Semantic search improves developer productivity by reducing context switching
